1-naive.raw_response:
Here are the FHIR queries to find patients with Down syndrome using common coding systems:

**Using SNOMED CT:**
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|35919005&_include=Condition:subject
```

**Using ICD-10-CM (all Down syndrome subtypes):**
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|Q90.0&_include=Condition:subject
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|Q90.1&_include=Condition:subject
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|Q90.2&_include=Condition:subject
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|Q90.9&_include=Condition:subject
```

**Notes:**
| Code | System | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `35919005` | SNOMED CT | Down syndrome (Trisomy 21) |
| `Q90.0` | ICD-10-CM | Trisomy 21, nonmosaicism |
| `Q90.1` | ICD-10-CM | Trisomy 21, mosaicism |
| `Q90.2` | ICD-10-CM | Trisomy 21, translocation |
| `Q90.9` | ICD-10-CM | Down syndrome, unspecified |

- `_include=Condition:subject` retrieves the associated `Patient` resources in the same response bundle.
- You may also append `&clinical-status=active` if you only want currently active diagnoses.

1-broad.raw_response:
## Down Syndrome — Complete Trisomy 21 FHIR Queries

**Relevant Codes:**
- **SNOMED CT** `41040004` — Complete trisomy 21 syndrome (Down syndrome)
- **ICD-10-CM** `Q90.0` — Trisomy 21, nonmosaicism (meiotic nondisjunction)

---

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|41040004&_include=Condition:subject
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|Q90.0&_include=Condition:subject
```

---

### Notes

| Parameter | Detail |
|---|---|
| `code` | Targets **complete (non-mosaic) trisomy 21** specifically |
| `_include=Condition:subject` | Pulls in the associated `Patient` resources in a single request |
| **SNOMED `41040004`** | *Complete trisomy 21 syndrome* — excludes mosaic (`409037005`) and translocation (`80908003`) subtypes |
| **ICD-10 `Q90.0`** | *Trisomy 21, nonmosaicism* — most specific non-mosaic ICD-10 code; consider also adding `Q90.9` (unspecified) if broader capture is acceptable |

> **Tip:** To retrieve only the patient list without condition details, chain the search:
> ```
> Patient?_has:Condition:patient:code=http://snomed.info/sct|41040004
> ```
1-broad.prompt_text: Find patients diagnosed with Down syndrome — caused by complete trisomy of chromosome 21.

1-expert.raw_response:
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|41040004
```

**Rationale:**

| Parameter | Value | Explanation |
|---|---|---|
| Resource | `Condition` | Clinical diagnosis records |
| `code` system | `http://snomed.info/sct` | SNOMED CT terminology |
| `code` value | `41040004` | **Complete trisomy 21 syndrome** — the precise SNOMED CT concept for Down syndrome caused by full nondisjunction |

**Why this exact code:**

- `41040004` — *Complete trisomy 21 syndrome* is the canonical SNOMED CT concept for classical Down syndrome (three full copies of chromosome 21), delivering the high diagnostic yield requested.
- **Intentionally excluded** per your requirements:
  - `35205006` — Trisomy 21 (broader, parent concept)
  - `254264002` — Partial trisomy 21 (translocation Down syndrome)
  - `205615000` — Mosaic trisomy 21
  - Any other chromosomal aneuploidy concepts

> ⚠️ **Note on hierarchy:** If your FHIR server supports SNOMED CT subsumption via `:below` modifier, *do not use* `Condition?code:below=...` here — that would inadvertently capture mosaic and partial trisomy variants. The single-code direct match (`41040004`) is the correct strategy for this phenotype as specified.

| `205615000` | SNOMED | Trisomy 21, meiotic nondisjunction |
| `205616004` | SNOMED | Trisomy 21, mitotic nondisjunction mosaicism |
| `254264002` | SNOMED | Partial trisomy 21 in Down's syndrome |
| `371045000` | SNOMED | Translocation Down syndrome |
| `Q90.0` | ICD-10-CM | Trisomy 21, nonmosaicism (meiotic nondisjunction) |
| `Q90.1` | ICD-10-CM | Trisomy 21, mosaicism (mitotic nondisjunction) |
| `Q90.2` | ICD-10-CM | Trisomy 21, translocation |
| `Q90.9` | ICD-10-CM | Down syndrome, unspecified |

| Code | System | Display | Included? |
|---|---|---|---|
| `41040004` | SNOMED CT | Complete trisomy 21 syndrome | ✅ |
| `205615000` | SNOMED CT | Trisomy 21, meiotic nondisjunction | ✅ (also complete trisomy) |
| `Q90.0` | ICD-10-CM | Trisomy 21, nonmosaicism (meiotic nondisjunction) | ✅ |
| `205616004` / `Q90.1` | SNOMED / ICD-10-CM | Mosaic trisomy 21 | ❌ excluded |
| `371045000` / `Q90.2` | SNOMED / ICD-10-CM | Translocation Down syndrome | ❌ excluded |
| `254264002` | SNOMED CT | Partial trisomy 21 | ❌ excluded |
| `Q90.9` | ICD-10-CM | Down syndrome, unspecified | ❌ excluded |

### Final FHIR Query

**FINAL ANSWER**

```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|41040004,http://snomed.info/sct|205615000,http://snomed.info/sct|205616004,http://snomed.info/sct|254264002,http://snomed.info/sct|371045000
```

**Codes included (all from VSAC "Down Syndrome (Disorders) SNOMED" value set):**

| SNOMED Code | Display |
|---|---|
| `41040004` | Complete trisomy 21 syndrome |
| `205615000` | Trisomy 21 – meiotic nondisjunction |
| `205616004` | Trisomy 21 – mitotic nondisjunction mosaicism |
| `254264002` | Partial trisomy 21 in Down's syndrome |
| `371045000` | Translocation Down syndrome |

All subtypes are unioned in a single comma-separated `code=` parameter (Playbook 1 + 8). ICD-10-CM codes (Q90.x) were tested and returned 0 hits, so only SNOMED is needed on this server.

| Code System | Code | Display | Count |
|---|---|---|---|

| ICD-10-CM | `Q90.0` | Trisomy 21, nonmosaicism (meiotic nondisjunction) | 0 (not used) |

- The server **exclusively uses SNOMED CT** for this condition — no ICD-10-CM records found.
- `41040004` ("Complete trisomy 21 syndrome") is the **exact SNOMED concept** for the complete (non-mosaic) trisomy 21 form the user specified.
- Subsumption confirmed: `41040004` **subsumes** `205615000` (meiotic nondisjunction subtype), so a single code captures all complete trisomy 21 cases — no need to union with the subtype code.
- Excluded mosaic (`205616000`), partial (`254264002`), and translocation (`371045000`) subtypes, as requested.

Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|41040004
